item.title: What I Learned From Setting Up An Online Bookstore With Wordpress Plugins
item.description: Useful tips on how a writer can set up their own website, using very affordable Wordpress plugins.

item.title: Creating Drive Lights on GPIO - LINUX ONLY!
item.description: Github repository Drive_Lights Description If you are using a Raspberry PI or have a USB add on for your PC with GPIO pins ( MCP2221A USB to...

item.title: What Figma's 7.7% Drop Is Actually Telling You
item.description: Claude Design launched. Figma dropped 7.7%. Both camps of developer discourse missed the real signal - here's what the market is actually pricing in.

item.title: A Private Recommendation System I Can Control | Frank
item.description: Mainstream feeds serve platform dwell time, not my brain. Pure RSS hands control back to me but doesn't solve the efficiency problem. What I want is a home cook — I pick the ingredients, it handles the cooking.

item.title: Hardware is hard? | Pradeep Khileri
RAG As Similarity Engine | Necro Notes
The Work Runs on Different Maps | Yusuf Aytas
item.description: The org chart shows formal accountability. It rarely shows how work actually moves. Expertise, decision paths, institutional memory, and boundary-spanning people shape execution far more than most plans admit.

item.title: A decade of software – Lennard Berger’s blog
item.description: Reflections on my first decade in the profession of software engineering.

item.title: Amplifying — Coding Agent Intelligence
item.description: What dev tools do Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor actually recommend? Systematic research across real repos and model generations.

item.title: My first impressions on ROCm and Strix Halo
item.description: Here I'll share my first impressions with ROCm and Strix Halo and how I've set up everything.128GB efficiently shared between the CPU and GPU.OS choice and driver installationI'm used to working with …

item.title: On the cult of meetings
Vercel confirms breach as hackers claim to be selling stolen data
item.description: Cloud development platform Vercel has disclosed a security incident after threat actors claimed to have breached its systems and are attempting to sell stolen data.

item.title: The spacetime of code · kolu
item.description: Complexity creeps along two axes — space and time. Hickey catches one; Löwy catches the other. A single-lens review only audits half the code.

item.title: Andrew Damon-Smith — datapnt
item.description: Personal blog and project notes from Andrew Damon-Smith — machine learning engineering, inference infrastructure, and tools for builders.
item.author: (Andrew Damon-Smith)

item.title: Context Is Software, Weights Are Hardware — Aravind Jayendran
item.description: Both KV cache and weights modulate activations. Context is software running on frozen hardware. Weight updates redesign the hardware itself. The difference matters more than most people think.

item.title: Mythos: Explained by a Retired Microsoft Engineer
item.description: Dave Plummer explains everything you should know about Mythos from Anthropic and the implications for cybersecurity and software!
item.author: (Dave's Garage)

item.title: Vercel Says Internal Systems Hit in Breach - Decipher
item.description: The incident came to light on Sunday and the company says it has brought in an incident response provider to investigate the intrusion. Details of the intrusion are scant at this point.

item.title: Vercel April 2026 security incident | Vercel Knowledge Base
item.description: We’ve identified a security incident that involved unauthorized access to certain internal Vercel systems.

item.title: Any Color You Like: NIST Scientists Create ‘Any Wavelength’ Lasers in Tiny Circuits for Light
item.description: NIST scientists and collaborators have pioneered a way to make integrated circuits for light by depositing complex patterns of specialized materials onto silicon wafers.

item.title: Thoughts and Feelings around Claude Design · Sam Henri Gold
